meanings.usage: 指出身高贵、有贵族头衔的人
负责看守囚犯的人，同 jailer
被关押的人，指因犯罪而被拘禁
戏剧、音乐等在观众面前的正式演出
在戏剧或电影中指定演员扮演某个角色
强烈要求某事发生，含有坚持己见之意
没有写字或图案的，指纸张等表面无内容
眼睛微微眯起以看得更清楚或避免强光
用于逗乐的话语或行为
监狱中关押单个囚犯的小房间
正式地将某物交给某人
表示立即或很快地做某事
监狱窗户或门上的金属条，用于防止逃跑

examples.en: Students must learn these poems by heart.
examples.zh: 学生们必须把这些诗背下来。

examples.en: He knew all the lines by heart.
examples.zh: 他把这些台词都背熟了。

examples.en: The show has run for years on end.
examples.zh: 这个节目已经连续演出了很多年。

examples.en: She practiced the piano night after night.
examples.zh: 她夜复一夜地练习钢琴。

examples.en: You have no cause to complain.
The actress came on to the stage at the second act.
examples.zh: 女演员在第二幕登上舞台。

phrases.usage: 捉弄某人；开某人的玩笑

examples.en: They decided to play a joke on their teacher.
examples.zh: 他们决定捉弄一下老师。

examples.en: The audience looked on eagerly as the drama unfolded.
examples.zh: 观众热切地看着剧情展开。

phrases.phrase: be cast in the role of

examples.en: She was cast in the role of Juliet.
examples.zh: 她被选中扮演朱丽叶的角色。

examples.en: He handed the letter to the prisoner.
With this, he hurried off the stage.
examples.zh: 说完，他便匆匆离开了舞台。

grammar.title: 一般过去时 (Simple Past Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示过去发生的动作或存在的状态
grammar.usage: 用于叙述过去发生的事情，故事通常采用过去时叙述

examples.en: The curtain went up on the final act.
examples.zh: 最后一幕的幕布升起了。

examples.en: The gaoler appeared with the precious letter.
examples.zh: 看守拿着那封珍贵的信出现了。

examples.en: The noble stared at the blank sheet of paper.
examples.zh: 贵族盯着那张空白的纸看了一会儿。

grammar.title: 过去进行时 (Past Continuous Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示过去某一时刻或时间段正在进行的动作
grammar.usage: 用于描述过去正在发生的事情，常与过去进行时连用

examples.en: The gaoler was looking on eagerly.
The aristocrat was sitting alone behind bars.
examples.zh: 贵族独自坐在铁栏后面。

grammar.title: used to 的用法
grammar.definition: 表示过去习惯性的动作或状态
grammar.structure: 主语 + used to + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 表示过去的习惯，与现在形成对比

examples.en: He always insisted that it should be written out in full.
examples.zh: 他总是坚持要求把信完整地写出来。

examples.en: The gaoler would always come on to the stage with a letter.
examples.zh: 看守总是拿着信走上舞台。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Some plays are so successful that + 主语 + 动词

original.en: Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end.
original.zh: 有些戏剧如此成功，以至于它们连续上演多年。

imitations.en: He is so tired that he can hardly speak.
imitations.zh: 他太累了，几乎说不出话来。

imitations.en: The book is so interesting that everyone wants to read it.
imitations.zh: 这本书太有趣了，每个人都想看。

sentencePatterns.pattern: One would expect + 主语 + to do something

original.en: One would expect them to know their parts by heart.
original.zh: 人们会期望他们把台词背熟。

imitations.en: One would expect a professional to do better.
imitations.zh: 人们会期望一个专业人士做得更好。

imitations.en: One would expect her to arrive on time.
imitations.zh: 人们会期望她准时到达。

sentencePatterns.pattern: ...to find out if + 主语 + 过去式

original.en: The gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if he had managed to learn the contents of the letter by heart.
original.zh: 看守决定捉弄他的同事，想看看他是否已经把信的内容背熟了。

imitations.en: She called him to find out if he was coming.
imitations.zh: 她打电话给他，想看看他是否要来。

imitations.en: I checked the paper to find out if I had passed.
imitations.zh: 我查看成绩单，想知道我是否通过了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Much to + 名词 + 动词

original.en: Much to the aristocrat's amusement, the gaoler returned...
original.zh: 令贵族非常高兴的是，看守回来了...

imitations.en: Much to my surprise, he won the competition.
imitations.zh: 令我非常惊讶的是，他赢得了比赛。

imitations.en: Much to their disappointment, it rained on the day of the picnic.
imitations.zh: 令他们失望的是，野餐那天下雨了。
